expectations.text: Rewrites story 1 without leaking implementation details like 'template engine' or 'S3' into the narrative
expectations.evidence: Story 1 narrative: 'As a learner, I can download a PDF certificate once I complete a course, so that I have a shareable record of my achievement.' Template engine and S3 are not in the narrative; they are mentioned only in the What Changed note as items removed.

expectations.text: Flags story 2 as a Fake Story / Task / not a user story
expectations.evidence: Story 2 title: 'Download button on completion page (flagged — reads as a task)'. Body: '⚠ **Misleading Story (Solution Trap)** — the original draft ... is phrased entirely as an implementation task ... If the real intent was purely "wire up the button" as a dev task, this should be tracked as a Task, not a story'. The response explicitly labels it as a task.

expectations.text: Splits story 3 into multiple independent stories (download, share, print, email)
expectations.evidence: Splits into 3a (Download PDF), 3b (Share to LinkedIn), 3c (Print-friendly layout), 3d (Email to chosen recipient) under 'Story 3 — split'.

expectations.text: Uses 'I can' throughout; no 'I want' remaining
expectations.evidence: All narratives use 'I can' (e.g., 'I can download a PDF certificate', 'I can share my certificate to my LinkedIn profile'). No 'I want' appears in any rewritten story.

expectations.text: Every rewritten story has an explicit Title
expectations.evidence: Each story begins with '**Title:**' (e.g., '**Title:** Download completion certificate as PDF', '**Title:** Share certificate to LinkedIn', etc.).

expectations.text: Replaces generic 'user' in story 1 with a concrete role (e.g., 'learner')
expectations.evidence: Story 1 uses 'As a learner'. What-changed note: 'Role shifted from "user" to "learner" per house style.'

expectations.text: Every story's INVEST check lists each of the six principles (I, N, V, E, S, T) individually with its own verdict — no compact 'all criteria met' one-liner
expectations.evidence: Every story uses the compact one-liner form: '✓ INVEST: all criteria met.' (Stories 1, 3a, 3c, 3d) or omits INVEST entirely (Story 2 has no INVEST block; 3b has only an anti-pattern note, no INVEST).

expectations.text: Each rewritten story has an inline 'What Changed' block (or similar per-story change note) directly following its INVEST check — not a single trailing summary table covering all stories
expectations.evidence: Stories 1 and 2 have per-story '_What I changed:_' notes. Stories 3a, 3b, 3c, 3d have no individual What-Changed blocks — instead a single '_What I changed on Story 3 overall:_' is placed after 3d, and a 'Summary of edits' bullet list closes the response. 3a, 3b, 3c in particular lack any per-story change note.

expectations.text: Does not reference system components, pages, endpoints, or concepts absent from the input drafts (e.g., no invented 'course-completion page', 'completion dashboard', or other named surfaces the user didn't write)
expectations.evidence: Story 2 narrative invents 'course-completion page' ('I can trigger the certificate download directly from the course-completion page') — the input draft said 'the completion page'. The assertion explicitly calls out 'course-completion page' as an example of an invented surface.
